_documentation.description: ADO Service Hook configuration for sending GHAzDO alerts to a Teams Workflow webhook. Create TWO service hook subscriptions: one for alert creation and one for state changes.
_documentation.navigation: Azure DevOps → Project Settings → Service hooks → + Create subscription → Web Hooks
_documentation.note: The webhook URL is generated by the Teams Workflow after saving. Copy it from the workflow trigger step.

subscriptions.name: GHAzDO Alert Created → Teams Workflow
_setupSteps: 1. Go to Azure DevOps → Your Project → Project Settings (gear icon)
4. Select 'Web Hooks' as the service type
6. For Trigger, select 'Code scanning alert created' (advancedsecurity.alert.created)
7. Set Repository to '[Any]' or select specific repo
10. Paste the Teams Workflow webhook URL in the URL field
13. Click 'Test' to send a test notification
14. Verify the test succeeds, then click 'Finish'

subscriptions.name: GHAzDO Alert State Changed → Teams Workflow (Auto-Close)
_setupSteps: 1. Create a SECOND service hook subscription (same steps as above)
2. For Trigger, select 'Code scanning alert state changed' (advancedsecurity.alert.statechanged)
3. Paste the AUTO-CLOSE workflow webhook URL (different from the create workflow)
4. This subscription sends events when alerts are fixed/dismissed/reopened
subscriptions._note: Only create this subscription if you've built the optional auto-close workflow (Step 6 in README)

_troubleshooting.testFails: Verify the webhook URL is correct and the Teams Workflow is enabled (not paused)
_troubleshooting.noWorkItem: Check the Teams Workflow run history for errors — the most common issue is an expired ADO connector sign-in
_troubleshooting.duplicateHooks: Each service hook creates a separate subscription. Delete old ones if you're recreating
_troubleshooting.permissions: You need 'Edit subscriptions' permission on the project (Project Administrator role)
